name: Proto-Ethiopian-Semitic (family node)
notes: STRUCTURAL ANCESTRY NODE (not a modelled G2P target). Ethiopian Semitic is the South-Semitic sub-branch (under West Semitic) comprising Geʽez and the modern Amharic and Tigrinya. It exists here only so the ancestry / phylogenetic-distance metrics of the modern varieties that reference it have a node to resolve to; NO phonology is claimed or reconstructed and the minimal grapheme set is a placeholder (afa.json / trk.json stub pattern).

sources.author: Weninger, S. (ed.)
sources.title: The Semitic Languages: An International Handbook
sources.notes: Reference for Ethiopian Semitic branching (structural node only).
